interactive.rationale: Page is a static code-card — no buttons, inputs, canvas, or event listeners that the user can drive.

references.rationale: The 'see also' section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5183927385587712 — matches the feature.

design_system.rationale: Inline styles route through --bg/--text/--accent variables, no raw hex, and button overrides pair background + color correctly.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept's lede mirrors the feature index promise — same nouns, same scope.

spec_match.rationale: Concept exercises the keywords the spec/feature-index lede surfaces — covers the headline API surface.

edge_cases.rationale: Page only walks the happy path — there is no visible branch for failure, denial, or invalid input.

openQuestions.title: Replace the static code-card with a real interactive surface
openQuestions.detail: CLAUDE.md rule #7 forbids pure code-snippet cards. Add a 'try it' button that invokes the API in-page and surfaces the return value or rendered side effect, with a graceful fallback when the API is missing.

openQuestions.title: Add a direct spec link
openQuestions.detail: References list only points back to the feature index or ChromeStatus. Add a direct link to the relevant spec (W3C draft, WICG explainer, or RFC) so readers can reach the normative source in one click.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate at least one failure mode
openQuestions.detail: The page currently has no failure branch. Add a visible second branch that exercises an error path: unsupported browser, permission denied, malformed input, or quota-exceeded. The contrast is what teaches the spec.

summary: Needs work: 2 rubric items fail. Address the major open questions before counting this as a finished concept.
